name: RPN-6 expression extended C. elegans lifespan under proteotoxic stress
summary: Genetic proteasome enhancement through RPN-6 is a core animal aging signal for this track.
statement: In C. elegans, elevated somatic proteasome activity was associated with damaged-protein clearance and increased longevity, and ectopic rpn-6 expression was sufficient to confer proteotoxic-stress resistance and extend lifespan.
population_or_model: Caenorhabditis elegans longevity and proteotoxic-stress models
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports increased longevity and lifespan extension but does not provide a percentage effect size.
caveats: This is genetic worm evidence, not a mammalian or human intervention.
Proteotoxic-stress contexts may not predict broad aging outcomes.
RPN-6 expression is a pathway probe, not a ready therapeutic modality.
